description:
You are a performance-tuning engineer who wants to begin collecting lightweight CPU-usage snapshots every five minutes.  
    1. Make sure the directory “/home/user/cron” exists (create it if it is missing).  
    2. Inside that directory create the file “/home/user/cron/myapp_profiler.cron”.  
       • The file **must contain exactly four lines** — including the required blank line — and **no leading or trailing spaces** on any line.  
       • The lines must be **precisely**:

SHELL=/bin/bash
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin

*/5 * * * * /usr/bin/top -b -n 1 >> /home/user/cron/cpu_usage.log 2>&1

         (Notice the completely blank third line; keep it.)  
    3. When the cron-file has been created, write a one-line status file “/home/user/cron/creation_status.log” that contains exactly:

DONE

       (uppercase, followed by a newline and nothing else).  

An automated test will:
    • Check that /home/user/cron/myapp_profiler.cron exists.  
    • Verify that its content exactly matches the four-line block above (byte-for-byte).  
    • Confirm that /home/user/cron/creation_status.log exists and contains the single word “DONE” followed by a newline.  

Accomplish everything with a single compound shell command if you like, but you may use as many interactive commands as you find convenient.
truth:
Expected final state for verification:

1. Directory:
   /home/user/cron  — permissions may remain default (agent will create).

2. File:
   Path: /home/user/cron/myapp_profiler.cron  
   Exact byte content (including terminating newline on each line):

SHELL=/bin/bash\n
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin\n
\n
*/5 * * * * /usr/bin/top -b -n 1 >> /home/user/cron/cpu_usage.log 2>&1\n

3. File:
   Path: /home/user/cron/creation_status.log  
   Exact byte content:
DONE\n
